content:
• Characters or elements that align with astrological archetypes, interacting in ways that mimic celestial movements.
• A potential narrative cycle tied to the zodiac, allowing the story to reset or evolve over a year.
5. Narrative Structure Across Mediums:
• Since your work involves various mediums, consider how they intersect:
• Text: Displays and erases based on reader engagement, generating an experience akin to fleeting memory.
• Audio: A persistent undercurrent that reacts to reader pace and pauses, building tension or dissolving into entropy.
• Visuals: Constantly shifting, reflecting narrative choices or the broader themes of creation and decay.

Your desire to use a combined linguistic evolution—where text becomes an interplay of symbols, abbreviations, and ancient scripts—can enhance immersion. A structured syntax where visual and phonetic elements merge (e.g., using emojis or QR-style glyphs to stand in for concepts) could make the experience uniquely alien yet relatable.

Ultimately, your work could function as an ever-evolving digital artifact, mirroring the way myths and narratives change and adapt over millennia.
